content.assessment: This proposal leaves the track structure and selection age untouched, meaning it does not engage cohort decline or spatial distribution as design parameters at all; it is demographically inert by design. This is neither a strength nor weakness in itself, but it means the proposal offers no lever against the mechanical class-size viability problem (F-live-demography-05) or the regionally uneven contraction already documented (F-live-demography-04). Compensatory funding targeted at feeder/disadvantaged schools could, however, indirectly interact with rural viability by shoring up institutions that are simultaneously losing students to selective tracks and facing demographic contraction, potentially slowing closures. The aging teacher workforce finding (F-live-demography-09) is directly relevant here: the proposal's teacher-incentive-funding lever (Act LII) could, if evaluated and strengthened, address exactly the disadvantaged/rural staffing shortage that compounds demographic strain, though the proposal does not present evidence that this channel has been assessed for that purpose.
opportunities: Targeted funding could be explicitly weighted toward regions already showing severe demographic contraction, converting a structure-blind proposal into one with spatial sensitivity
strengths: Non-structural approach avoids adding further complexity to an already numerically fragile multi-track system in shrinking rural areas

Teacher-incentive funding channel (Act LII) could plausibly be redirected toward the documented rural/disadvantaged staffing shortage (F-live-demography-09), a pre-existing lever this proposal is positioned to strengthen
threats: If cohort decline continues on its embedded 20+ year trajectory (F-live-demography-02) without accompanying structural change, compensatory funding alone may be overwhelmed by the scale of rural school viability collapse implied by settlements with only 50-70 school-age children (F-live-demography-04)
weaknesses: Does not address the mechanical class-size constraint (F-live-demography-05) or the specific regional contraction patterns (F-live-demography-04) at all, since track structure remains unchanged
No engagement with counterfactual contraction dynamics; the proposal is silent on what happens to feeder schools' viability as cohorts shrink independent of funding changes
